title: QuickScope
summary: Bayesian-optimal evaluation for finding and certifying hard regions in dynamic LLM benchmarks.
description: QuickScope adaptively allocates evaluation budget across a benchmark's configuration space, helping identify model weak spots without uniformly evaluating every generated question. It is built around COUP-style Bayesian optimization and supports pluggable datasets, utilities, and benchmark generators.

resources.label: code
resources.title: Code
resources.description: Python CLI and library for adaptive evaluation, fixed-budget baselines, offline datasets, result summaries, and utility plots.

resources.label: paper
resources.title: Paper
resources.description: Introduces the method for certifying hard questions in dynamic benchmarks, with modifications that make Bayesian optimization practical for LLM evaluation pipelines.

relatedLinks.label: DyVal
relatedLinks.title: DyVal Benchmark
relatedLinks.description: Dynamic evaluation benchmark supported by QuickScope's built-in DyVal scenario.

relatedLinks.label: BOCOEL
relatedLinks.title: BOCOEL
relatedLinks.description: Related Bayesian-optimization toolkit for reducing the cost of evaluating language models.

title: STEER Benchmark
summary: Benchmark family for evaluating economic reasoning in language models across strategic and non-strategic settings.
description: STEER is a benchmark family for evaluating economic reasoning in language models. STEER focuses on strategic economic settings with multiple-choice questions, while STEER-ME extends the suite to non-strategic microeconomic reasoning and includes both multiple-choice and free-text formats.

resources.label: dataset
resources.title: Dataset
resources.description: Hugging Face dataset for STEER-ME benchmark artifacts.

resources.label: STEER paper
resources.title: STEER Paper
resources.description: Paper introducing the STEER benchmark for assessing economic rationality in large language models.

resources.label: STEER-ME paper
resources.title: STEER-ME Paper
resources.description: Paper introducing STEER-ME for assessing microeconomic reasoning in large language models.

relatedPublications.title: EconEvals: Benchmarks and Litmus Tests for Economic Decision-Making by LLM Agents

title: Auto-STEER
summary: Template-based pipeline for building economics benchmark suites from human-written seeds.
description: Auto-STEER is the generation and testing pipeline behind STEER-ME. Given human-written seeds in the expected format, it supports creating numerical variations, checking generated instances, and producing full suites of multiple-choice and free-text economics questions.

resources.label: code
resources.title: Code
resources.description: Generation and testing pipeline for authoring benchmark templates, producing numerical variations, and assembling economics question suites.

resources.label: paper
resources.title: STEER-ME Paper
resources.description: Paper describing the benchmark and generation methodology supported by Auto-STEER.

relatedPublications.title: GSM-Symbolic: Understanding the Limitations of Mathematical Reasoning in Large Language Models
